title: PageLayout — Standard Page Wrapper with Back Button
problem: Every deep-navigation page (clip detail, band settings, editor, band creation) needs a standard header with a back button and page title, plus correct bottom padding to clear the fixed tab bar and device safe area. Without a shared PageLayout, every page reimplements this boilerplate differently.
analysis:
PageLayout renders a standard page structure:
- Fixed header: back button (← + screen name as a Next.js `<Link>`, not `<a>`), page title
- Scrollable content area with `pb-[calc(56px+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))]` to clear tab bar + home indicator
- Optional right-side header action slot (e.g., '+ New Session' button)

Back button uses Next.js `<Link href={backHref}>` for client-side navigation — no full page reload on back tap.
successCriteria:
- Header renders with back button (chevron-left icon + back label) and page title
- Back button navigates via Next.js Link (client-side, no reload)
- Content scrollable, does not hide behind tab bar
- pb-[calc(56px+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))] on content area
- Optional right header slot rendered when provided
- Compatible with AuthShell (does not add its own UploadBanner or BottomTabBar)
implementation: components/layout/PageLayout.tsx: props { title, backHref, backLabel?: string, headerRight?: ReactNode, children }. Renders <header> (sticky top-0 bg-elevated z-10), <main className='pb-[calc(56px+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))]'>{children}</main>.

decisions.decision: Back button uses Next.js Link, not <a href> or router.back()
decisions.rationale: <a href> triggers full page reload. router.back() goes to the previous history entry which may not be the intended parent page. An explicit backHref with <Link> is predictable and SSR-compatible.
decisions.recommendation: PageLayout requires explicit backHref prop. Callers provide the logical parent URL.

knownLimitations: pageTitle and backLabel are static — no dynamic updates after mount (e.g., updating the title as clip name is edited requires the caller to update props)
userGuide: Internal layout component. The back arrow at the top-left of each page uses this component to navigate back.
